commit.message:
anime: VIP URL resolution + better logging + site diagnostics endpoint

- Add tryResolveEpisodeUrl(): extracts direct stream URL from VIP player wrapper URLs
  (common pattern: https://jx.example.com/?url=https://cdn.example.com/video.m3u8)
- parseAllRoutes() now calls tryResolveEpisodeUrl() on every episode URL
- /api/tv/stream proxy: on HTML response, try extractVideoUrlFromHtml() before 422
  (some players embed the actual stream URL in JS variables or JSON)
- querySite() timeout: 5s -> 10s per site; hard cap: 15s -> 25s
- querySite() now logs every outcome (0 results, no match, no URL, success)
- Add GET /api/anime/test-sites?name=keyword diagnostic endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(colony-subscriber): Envoak schema compat — session auth, status query, field mapping

3 fixes for colony_state compatibility:

1. Auth: Add COLONY_SESSION_JWT env var. Use session JWT in Authorization
   header (anon key alone is blocked by RLS on colony_state view).
   apikey header still receives anon key for routing.

2. Query: colony_state?status=eq.in-progress (not colony_signals?type=work_claim).
   colony_state is deduped by colony_gc, is_stale computed server-side.

3. Field mapping: read task (branch), files[], agent_label as top-level
   columns from colony_state view — no payload parsing for these fields.
   payload.branch / payload.agent kept as backwards-compat fallbacks.

Release sweep updated: status-based model has no work_release type.
Branches absent from the colony in-progress set are swept from local registry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial release: nested repository support preset for Spec Kit

Adds multi-module nested repository support for independent repos and git
submodules during the plan and tasks phases of spec-driven development.

Features:
- Auto-scan for independent .git directories with configurable depth
- Git submodule detection via .gitmodules parsing
- Auto mode that detects which type applies
- .gitignore-aware directory filtering during scan
- Type-aware branch creation tasks (git -C vs git submodule update)
- Configuration via .specify/init-options.json (optional)
- Full core plan/tasks workflow preserved with additive changes only

* fix: add sudo to devcontainer install-tools.sh for /usr/local/bin writes

onCreateCommand runs as the vscode user which lacks write permissions
to /usr/local/bin. Add sudo to the syft, trivy, and golangci-lint
install commands.

all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

commit.message:
Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility (#1315)

* Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility

FastText library has been archived since March 2024 and is no longer
maintained. This dependency was blocking numpy 2.x adoption.

Changes:
- Remove fasttext extra from setup.py dependencies
- Add deprecation warnings to fasttext.py module
- Update documentation to recommend langdetect as primary option
- Update CLI to deprecate --fasttext flag
- Add comprehensive test suite for langdetect integration
- Remove fasttext from tox.ini test configurations

Users should migrate to langdetect:
  pip install dateparser[langdetect]

Closes #1287
Closes #1251

* Update dateparser_cli/cli.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update dateparser/custom_language_detection/fasttext.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify fasttext deprecation to always error

The previous approach was confusing:
- If fasttext was installed, it showed a warning but still worked
- If fasttext wasn't installed, it raised an ImportError

This creates inconsistent deprecation - users don't know if the
feature is truly deprecated or not.

Now the module:
- Always shows deprecation warning on import
- Always raises ImportError when detect_languages() is called
- Provides clear migration path to langdetect
- No functional fasttext code remains

This makes the deprecation clear and consistent.

* Remove unused module-level mock in test_language_detect

The module-level 'detect_languages' mock was defined but never used.
The MockLangDetectTest class defines its own mock as a class attribute,
and other test classes use the actual lang_detect_detect_languages function.

This cleanup removes the unused mock to avoid confusion.

commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

commit.message:
fix(hostexec): resolve relative argv0 against cwd for rule matching

When an agent runs `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply`, the Python
wrapper sends argv0="./gradlew" with cwd="/workspace/backend". The rule
defines argv0="./backend/gradlew" (workspace-root-relative). matchRule
compared these directly and failed to match, causing a fallback error:
"relative argv0 fallback is not supported".

Fix matchRule to accept an optional MatchContext (cwd + workspaceRoot).
When direct relative-path comparison fails, resolve the actual argv0
against cwd to an absolute path, then re-derive the workspace-relative
path for comparison. This is done in the broker (TS) where both cwd and
workspaceRoot are already available, avoiding changes to the Python
wrapper or new environment variables.
